storage_extensions.usage: mmap codes.bin; read plabel_codes[pre_row] as integer group key component for IS-range pre rows during hash map build. Decode plabel strings only for final 200 output rows.

join_order.notes: Bitset indexed by sub_fk for O(1) filter during num scan

Build a COMPACT open-addressing hash multimap at runtime from IS-range pre rows only. Key=(sub_fk, tag_code, version_code) packed into uint64_t, value=plabel_code. DO NOT use the pre_by_adsh_tag_ver.idx file. This replaces 275MB random-access index probes + stmt_code + plabel_code random reads with a single ~30-40MB cache-resident structure.

data_structures.sub_fk_to_name_id: direct_array(uint32_t, 86135) — maps sub_fk to deduplicated name_id
data_structures.is_pre_hashmap: Open-addressing hash multimap built at runtime from IS-filtered pre rows. Use stmt_offsets to get IS row range in pre table. Scan that range, read pre/sub_fk.bin, pre/tag_code.bin, pre/version_code.bin, and plabel codes.bin for those rows. Pack key as uint64_t: ((uint64_t)sub_fk << 34) | ((uint64_t)tag_code << 17) | version_code (sub_fk max 86135=17bits, tag_code max 198311=18bits, version_code max 83815=17bits, total 52 bits). Store plabel_code as uint32_t value. Use struct {uint64_t key; uint32_t plabel_code; uint32_t padding;} = 16 bytes/slot. For multi-value handling: when inserting a duplicate key with different plabel_code, use linear probing to find next empty slot and store as separate entry (same key, different value). During probe, scan all consecutive matching keys. Capacity: next power of 2 above 1.06M * 2 = ~4M slots × 16 bytes = 64MB. To fit LLC better, use load factor 0.5: 2M slots × 16 bytes = 32MB. Initialize with EMPTY_KEY sentinel.
data_structures.aggregation: open_addressing_hash_map<GroupKey(name_id:u32, tag_code:u32, plabel_code:u32) packed into uint64_t → AggValue(sum:double, count:u64)>, thread-local with merge. Same packKey as iter_1.

parallelism.notes: Morsel size 100K rows over USD range. Thread-local agg maps, merge after scan. The is_pre_hashmap is SHARED read-only across all threads (built once before parallel scan). Software prefetch: for each num row, prefetch the is_pre_hashmap slot for a row PREFETCH_DIST ahead.

indexes.purpose: Get contiguous USD row range in num
Get contiguous IS row range in pre for building compact hash map

optimization_notes: Category B (algorithm change): The main_scan phase (~90ms) is bottlenecked by random probes into the 275MB pre_by_adsh_tag_ver.idx file, which is 6x larger than the 44MB LLC. Each of ~6.2M qualifying num rows triggers random reads into: (1) the 275MB index, (2) stmt_code array (9.6MB), and (3) plabel_codes array (38MB) — total ~322MB random-access working set. The fix: stop using the pre-built index entirely. Instead, build a compact open-addressing hash multimap at runtime from only the IS-filtered pre rows (~1.06M rows). Pack the 3-component key into a single uint64_t (52 bits). The resulting hash map is ~32MB and fits in LLC. During the num scan, each qualifying row does a single probe into this cache-resident structure instead of 3 random accesses into cache-exceeding arrays. Expected speedup: 2-3x on main_scan phase from eliminating L3 cache misses. Build cost: ~5-10ms for sequential scan of 1.06M pre rows (sequential I/O, trivially fast).
